content.problemStatement:
Chef wants to give a gift to Chefina to celebrate their anniversary. Of course, he has a sequence a1,a2,…,aNa_1, a_2, \ldots, a_Na1​,a2​,…,aN​ ready for this occasion. Since the half-heart necklace is kind of cliche, he decided to cut his sequence into two pieces and give her a piece instead. Formally, he wants to choose an integer lll (1≤l<N1 \le l \lt N1≤l<N) and split the sequence into its prefix with length lll and its suffix with length N−lN-lN−l.

Chef wants his gift to be cute; he thinks that it will be cute if the product of the elements in Chefina's piece is coprime with the product of the elements in his piece. Can you tell him where to cut the sequence? Find the smallest valid lll such that Chef's gift would be cute.
constraints: the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 3⋅1053 \cdot 10^53⋅105
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 600600600
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 6,0006,0006,000
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains the integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers a1,a2,…,aNa_1, a_2, \ldots, a_Na1​,a2​,…,aN​.
content.outputFormat:
For each test case, print a single line containing one integer lll where Chef should cut the sequence.

It is guaranteed that a solution exists for the given test data.
